claimText: A river stretch is identified as a Polluted River Stretch (PRS) if its Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) exceeds 3 milligrams/litre (mg/L).
evidenceExcerpt: The primary criterion for identifying a Polluted River Stretch is a Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) exceeding 3 mg/L.
examUse: Statement-based question on water quality criteria and pollution indicators.

claimText: Polluted River Stretches are categorized into five priority classes (I to V) based on the maximum Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) observed.
evidenceExcerpt: PRS are categorized into Five Priority Classes (I to V) based on the maximum BOD observed.
examUse: Statement-based question on the classification methodology of polluted rivers.
distractorHooks: Categorized based on heavy metal concentration
Categorized based on the length of the polluted stretch

claimText: The total number of Polluted River Stretches (PRS) in India has decreased between 2018 and 2025.
evidenceExcerpt: The total number of PRS in India has decreased from 351 in 2018 to 296 in 2025.
examUse: Statement-based question on environmental trends regarding river pollution in India.
distractorHooks: The number of PRS has continuously increased since 2018
The number of PRS has remained stagnant over the last decade

claimText: Maharashtra has the highest number of Polluted River Stretches in India.
evidenceExcerpt: Maharashtra has the highest number of Polluted River Stretches (54) in India.
examUse: Fact-based or correct-pair question on state-wise river pollution data.
distractorHooks: Uttar Pradesh has the highest number of PRS
West Bengal has the highest number of PRS
Bihar has the highest number of PRS
